# Copilot instructions

<!-- generated by Project Spine; edit `brief.md` or rerun `spine compile` rather than hand-editing -->

**Project Spine** — Ship a deterministic context compiler that turns a client brief + a repo + optional design inputs into a repo-native operating layer for coding agents (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / copilot-instructions.md plus a full scaffold plan).

**Stack:** node-library • typescript • tests: vitest • pm: npm

## Repo conventions

- TypeScript strict mode is on. Never use `any`; prefer `unknown` + narrow at the boundary.
- Use `npm` for all dependency operations. Do not commit lockfiles from other package managers.

## Do / Don't

- Prefer small, focused diffs. One concern per PR or patch.
- Always show the exact test or typecheck command you ran and its result.
- When following a rule from this file, cite it by section heading so reviewers can audit.
- Do not refactor code unrelated to the task, even if it looks better.
- Do not add new dependencies without flagging the choice and reason.

## Never do this

- Never modify generated files (anything under `dist/`, `build/`, `.next/`, or marked `@generated`).
- Never edit lockfiles manually.
- Never commit secrets. Redact any `.env*` content that appears in logs or diffs.

## Quality bar

- Unit tests live next to source as `*.test.ts`. Run `vitest run` in CI and before every commit touching business logic.
- `tsc --noEmit` must pass on every PR.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: spine --help lists exactly the routed OSS commands — no hosted-tier leakage.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: Determinism test: recompiling with identical inputs yields a byte-identical spine.json and identical export hashes.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: Drift check exits non-zero when any input file's sha256 changes; spine drift diff prints the unified patch for every hand-edited export.
- Success criterion to verify before launch: First run (fresh clone) under 30 seconds from npm install → spine init → spine compile with a usable brief and the saas-marketing template.

_Full QA guardrails: `.project-spine/exports/qa-guardrails.md`._

## Accessibility

- All interactive elements must be reachable and operable with the keyboard alone.
- Focus must be visible at all times; never remove outlines without replacing them.
- Text contrast must meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text).
- Every form control has a programmatic label.
- Pages use proper landmark regions (header, main, nav, footer) and a sensible heading order.
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` for any non-essential animation.

_Source of truth: `.project-spine/spine.json` (hash `ce09cb93352dfefb`)._
